behavior_summary: U8P_BIGDEMST::look is a simple 'U8P_BIGDEMST::look' (examine) process for an object/NPC that displays a single short description text: "daemon statue".
After emitting the description (a Bark node), the process issues a suspend, so the U8P_BIGDEMST::look interaction pauses/ends until resumed by the engine.
There are no conditionals, menu interactions, flag reads/writes, or external calls in this excerpt — behavior is minimal and purely descriptive.
notable_control_flow: Single linear flow: on entry, present a bark ('daemon statue'), then suspend the process.
No branching, loops, or calls observed in the decompiled nodes — control ends with a suspend instruction.
open_questions: Is this process invoked only by a direct player 'U8P_BIGDEMST::look' (examine) action, or can the engine also call it via fast-area entry semantics in some contexts?
The decompilation contains two Unknown nodes around the Bark — could they represent logging, short delay, or engine bookkeeping before/after the Bark?
Does the suspend represent a permanent end-of-process for U8P_BIGDEMST::look handlers in this engine, or will some external resume occur later?
